task_completion.description: Open or view the raw airfoil CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, spreadsheet tool, or terminal showing the contents of 'project_falcon_foil.csv', which includes commas and extra columns like Index, Z-values, or Notes.

task_completion.description: Clean and format the data into Selig format
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the cleaned data: the exact text 'Project_Falcon_Foil' on the first line, followed by rows of just X and Y coordinates separated by whitespace. There must be no commas, indices, or Z-values visible in the cleaned data.

task_completion.description: Open QBlade and navigate to the Airfoil Design module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QBlade application window is open, and the active module or workspace is clearly set to 'Airfoil Design'.

task_completion.description: Import the cleaned airfoil data into QBlade
task_completion.visual_evidence: The airfoil 'Project_Falcon_Foil' is visible in QBlade's airfoil list/manager, or the airfoil shape is successfully plotted in the main graphical view of the Airfoil Design module.

task_completion.description: Save the QBlade project to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box showing the path '~/Documents/projects/' and the filename 'falcon_design.wpa', or the QBlade window title bar displaying 'falcon_design.wpa' after saving.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the QBlade GUI to import and save the project
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show actual interaction with the QBlade graphical interface (menus, import dialogs, save dialogs), not just terminal commands attempting to mock or touch a .wpa file.

integrity.description: Verify the imported data forms a valid airfoil
integrity.visual_evidence: The plot in QBlade should show a recognizable, continuous airfoil curve (S809 profile), indicating the 54 coordinate points were properly parsed, rather than a broken shape or a few dummy points.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not attempt to manually write text into the .wpa file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen using a text editor (like nano or vim) or echo commands to write plain text directly into 'falcon_design.wpa', as it is a proprietary project file format.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input CSV is located at /home/ga/Documents/incoming/project_falcon_foil.csv. The cleaned file must strictly follow Selig format: the exact name 'Project_Falcon_Foil' on the first line, followed by X and Y coordinates separated by whitespace, with absolutely no commas (,). The final QBlade project must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/projects/falcon_design.wpa.
